A.title: FINANCIALS
A.inline_verdict: Margin recovery operationally credible (Q3 FY25/26 ~13.5%) but 150bp R&D cap unwinding + Varian threat = consensus 13.5% EBIT FY26/27 vs management 14%+ target. FCF doubling structural; balance sheet comfortable. Order-book write-downs (SEK 7.1bn across 2 quarters) erode the revenue base against which margin leverage compounds.

items.text: FY24/25 actuals: revenue SEK 18,016M (+1% cc), adj EBITA margin 12.2% (+180bp YoY), reported EBIT depressed by SEK 1,064M impairment on discontinued cloud R&D — strip impairment to read trajectory.

sub.text: Net sales by segment: APAC ~SEK 5.8bn (32%), EMEA ~SEK 6.3bn (35%), Americas ~SEK 5.9bn (33%).
Service revenue grew 6% cc FY24/25; Solutions declined 2% cc — mix shift to recurring is real.

items.text: Q4 FY24/25 GM hit 40.3% — highest in 5 years — driven by Evo pricing realised in EMEA + software mix shift.

sub.text: Q3 FY25/26 GM 38.3% with 130bp FX drag + 100bp tariff drag — __underlying margin still rising__ once headwinds normalised.

items.text: **FCF inflection structural, not one-off:** SEK 1,248M FY24/25 (80% conversion); +SEK 443M YTD FY25/26 despite restructuring cash. NWC at -9% of sales (down from +0% historic).

sub.text: DSO compressed 82.6 → 76.0 days; DIO 101.8 → 97.4. Customer-advance model drives negative working capital.
Cash conversion 132% in Q3 FY25/26 — restructuring cash flowing through P&L, not balance sheet.

items.text: **R&D capitalisation policy unwinding** — historically 60% of ~SEK 1.2-1.4bn capitalised; shifting to 40%. Creates ~150bp persistent EBIT drag for 2-3Y as prior projects amortise.

sub.text: Q3 FY25/26 alone shows ~150bp drag from policy shift — consensus models 13.5% EBIT FY26/27 (not management's 14%+).

The shift is __accounting-quality positive but reported-margin negative__. Bears (Morgan Stanley) argue this exposes prior margin as artificially flattered. Bulls argue cleaner reporting is sustainable.

items.text: Balance sheet: net debt SEK 3,819M; net debt/EBITDA 1.35x; SEK 2,541M cash + untapped facilities; bond refinanced Oct 2024 (SEK 1.5B). No constraint on restructuring or buybacks.
**Triple-lever bridge to 14% EBIT is __mechanically possible but accounting-eroded__:** ~3pp from cost savings + ~50-80bp from SaaS mix + ~100-200bp Evo pricing (US-volume-dependent) —

sub.text: minus 150bp R&D cap unwinding minus 100-200bp Varian competitive risk = realistic range 12.5-13.5% by FY26/27, not 14%+.
Consensus EPS SEK 4.47, EBITDA SEK 3,807M, sales SEK 18,429M FY25/26. EPS revisions L1M -4%, L3M -5%, L6M flat — momentum bottoming, not turning.

B.title: EXECUTIVE SUMMARY
B.inline_verdict: PARK at ESA. Conviction C — forming, deteriorating bias. ESA depth surfaces 3 new high-stakes risks (Varian Sept-26, R&D cap unwinding, backlog cascade) that compress the bull bridge. Cost lever holds; product-cycle leg structurally compromised.

blocks.text: _PARK at ESA. ESA depth weakens the Triaging-stage 'progress' verdict — Varian Sept-26 next-gen platform + R&D cap unwinding (-150bp) + backlog cascade risk turn the two-leg case into a one-leg case. Cost lever real; product cycle compromised. Re-activate on Sept-26 Varian event OR 3 clean backlog quarters OR Just-Bomholt animal-CEO confirmed._

items.text: **Why PARK (vs Triaging's PROGRESS):** Triaging said 'two highest-stakes ESA-gate questions are CEO quality + US Evo ramp.' ESA finds CEO quality is workable (cost execution credible) but __US Evo

sub.text: ramp is structurally compromised__: revenue is FY27/28 not FY26/27; Varian Sept-26 platform threatens the 30% pricing premium within 8 months of FDA clearance. The case __failed the gate question Triaging set__.

items.text: **Three new ESA-stage findings driving the recommendation:** (1) __Varian Sept-26 next-gen platform__ — 60-70% probability of materially superior launch per VCAnalysis expert calls; ACH#12 NEW;

sub.text: (2) __R&D capitalisation unwinding 60%→40%__ — 150bp persistent reported-EBIT drag for 2-3Y; (3) __Backlog cascade risk__ — SEK 7.1bn write-downs across Q1+Q2 FY25/26; one clean Q3 print insufficient per Cockroach Rule (need 3+); ACH#11 NEW.

items.text: **What survives at ESA:** Cost program (SEK 500M, 80% executed) is the highest-conviction lever (~3pp EBIT). Just-Bomholt's Getinge precedent (300bp margin recovery 2022-24) is genuine. FCF doubling structural (NWC -9%).

sub.text: Balance sheet comfortable (1.35x net debt/EBITDA). Technicals 8/8 Minervini intact. __None of these are enough on their own to clear the entry bar at C-conviction with deteriorating bias.__

items.text: **What's broken at ESA:** IC#3 setup C → D (one-setup case at C-conviction does not cross entry bar); BB#2 Foundation C → D (Varian bundle moat + Philips magnet single-source

sub.text: + US share erosion structural); BB#4 Strenuously-seek-to-avoid C → D (3 of 5 avoid-patterns present); BB#6 Invalidating Constraints B → D (3 of 10 ACHs in WATCH including 2 NEW). __Rating distribution shifts visibly downward vs Triaging.__

items.text: **Re-activation plan (E.III.2 detail):** ANY ONE of: (a) Sept-26 Varian platform announcement is parity or incremental (not superior); (b) 3 consecutive clean backlog quarters (Q3+Q4 FY25/26 + Q1 FY26/27);

sub.text: (c) Just-Bomholt animal-CEO confirmed via major capital allocation action OR explicit FY27/28 14% margin commit at June-26 CMD with quantitative bridge. Auto-kill if any of: (i) third backlog write-down, (ii) Varian materially superior, (iii) Just-Bomholt departs.

blocks.text: _The ESA work earned its keep — it surfaced exactly the kind of structural risk pattern Triaging's flat coverage cannot see (Varian timing + accounting unwinding + cockroach pattern). PARK is the right answer; not because EKTA is uninvestable, but because three high-stakes binary events resolve in the next 4-7 months and committing capital before resolution is path-dependent in a way that disadvantages reward/risk. Re-assess at Sept-26 Varian event with cleaner information set._

blocks.text: _P3 = case-output focus. Three families: IC#1 OUTPUTS (margin/EPS bridge), IC#2 INPUTS (change forces verifiable), IC#3 SETUPS (Corporate Change + Product Cycle). Held at C; bias deteriorating; IC#3 downgraded._

_Outputs bridge mechanically intact (cost + Evo + SaaS = ~250-400bp gross uplift), but ESA depth identifies offsets (R&D cap unwinding -150bp, Varian competitive -100-200bp) that compress the realistic FY26/27 EBIT range to 12.5-13.5% vs the 14%+ target. Output rating C — the bridge is real but contested._

items.text: **IC#1 family verdict:** EPS bridge from SEK 4.50 (FY25/26 cons) → SEK 5.50-6.20 (FY27/28 modal) is __triple-lever certain on cost / single-lever fragile on revenue__.

sub.text: Cost-lever leg: SEK 500M run-rate, 80% executed, full flow Q1 FY26/27 — adds ~3pp EBIT. __Highest conviction.__
Mix-lever leg: SaaS at 80% GM, currently 26% of revenue, growing ~15%/yr. Adds ~50-80bp/yr to GM. __3-5Y compounder, not 18-24M catalyst.__
Pricing-lever leg: Evo +30% premium, US-volume-dependent, FY27/28 revenue event. __Most fragile (Varian threat — see C.II.2 P4 BB#3 below)__.

items.text: **TC1 EPS growth path credible?** Yes for FY26/27 (12% YoY consensus); contested for FY27/28 (range 8-25% across SS).

sub.text: Bull case (DNB): FY27/28 EPS SEK 6.50 (+22% on Evo + China). Bear case (MS): FY27/28 EPS SEK 4.20 (-7% on Varian erosion). Spread = 55%.

items.text: **TC2 Margin target 14%+ achievable? D — unlikely on current bridge.** R&D cap unwinding (-150bp) + Varian risk (-100-200bp) eats the cost lever (+300bp). Realistic FY26/27 = 12.5-13.5%.

sub.text: Management 14% target requires: (i) US Evo ramp aggressive, (ii) Varian Sept-26 absent or inferior, (iii) China recovery clean. Probability all three = ~25%.
__The 14% target is now a bull-case anchor, not a base case.__ Consensus at 13.5% modal already prices this; market not surprised by miss to 13%, but downgrade if <12.5%.

items.text: **TC3 FCF sustainable? C → B.** NWC -9% structural (customer-advance model); restructuring cash tail clears in FY26/27. Modal FCF SEK 1.8bn FY26/27 = 70-80% conversion.
**TC4 Capital return scope? C — limited.** Net debt 1.35x EBITDA leaves room but management priority is restructuring + bolt-on M&A (e.g., AI software). Buyback unlikely until FY27/28.

But ESA reveals a __new contested input__: Varian Sept-26 next-gen platform (probability 60-70%) which directly threatens the Evo input. C held; bias deteriorating._

But __operator-quality bear-case__: Q1+Q2 FY25/26 SEK 7.1bn write-downs in his first 2 quarters could be 'kitchen-sinking' (resetting base aggressively). Bullish read = decisive cleanup; bearish read = pre-emptive blame-laying. __Cannot resolve at ESA — needs DD-stage operator deep-dive.__

items.text: **TC2 Demand inputs intact? C.** US linac fleet 66% >8 years old (replacement-cycle real), but US capex cycle in pause-mode (interest rates, hospital margin pressure).

sub.text: Replacement cycle is structurally inevitable; timing is rate-cycle-dependent. Bull: 2026-27 capex thaw. Bear: 2027-28 thaw.
China recovery: Q2+Q3 FY25/26 double-digit cc order growth in China — but this is the SAME market that drove the SEK 7.1bn write-downs. Quality of new orders unverified.

items.text: **TC3 Competitive intensity stable? D — DETERIORATING.** Varian's Sept-26 next-gen platform is a __material competitive event__ that compresses Evo's commercial window to 8 months. (NEW finding at ESA —

sub.text: see D.II.1 ACH#11.)

Varian post-Siemens bundles linac + diagnostic imaging + ARIA OIS into enterprise-wide hospital contracts. Elekta cannot match the bundle. US share loss 20% → <15% over 3 years validates bundle is winning.

__Probability Varian launches on schedule (Sept-26) with materially superior platform: 60-70%__ per VCAnalysis expert calls. If realised, Evo's 30% pricing premium collapses; back to discount-bidding.

items.text: **TC4 Regulatory environment stable? B.** Evo FDA Jan-26; CE mark Q4-25; China NMPA mid-25. No regulatory invalidation visible.

items.text: **IC#3 family verdict — DOWNGRADE C→D.** Two-setup case is now a one-setup case (Corporate Change holds; Product Cycle compromised).

sub.text: Corporate Change EPSU/EPT setup: still __present and progressing__. Restructuring 80% executed, CEO credible on cost, FCF doubling.
Product Cycle EPSU setup: __compromised at ESA__. Evo revenue is FY27/28 (not FY26/27) + Varian Sept-26 risk + R&D cap unwinding eats reported margins.

__A one-setup case at C-conviction does not cross the entry bar__ unless the one setup is itself an A or B. Corporate Change here is B-grade execution but C-grade outcome (cost cuts insufficient to clear 14% target solo).

sub.text: Cost savings: SEK 500M = ~3pp EBIT, full Q1 FY26/27. China: book-to-bill 1.05 Q3 — first stabilisation, needs 3+ quarters to validate.
__Fulcrum is now defensive (cost + stabilisation), not offensive (Evo growth). Re-rating thesis weaker as a result.__

items.text: **TC2 Order book quality validated? F.** Two consecutive write-down quarters (SEK 4.9bn Q1 + SEK 2.2bn Q2 FY25/26 = 39% of annual revenue). Q3 was clean (1 quarter) — insufficient.

sub.text: Per memo doctrine v3.8 §IV.H entry #11 (Plain sight risks DOUBLE) — this is a plain-sight risk that __cannot be ignored at ESA__. Bull narrative requires backlog credibility; current data: 2 fail / 1 pass.

__Per AJ SOP §10 INVALIDATION ACHs and per APM SKILL §Cockroach Pattern__: 2 consecutive write-down quarters fits cockroach pattern. Cockroach rule: presume more cockroaches until 3+ clean quarters. Do not enter on 1 clean print.

items.text: **TC3 Catalyst calendar visible? C.** Q4 FY25/26 print May-26 (margin + write-down); June-26 CMD (FY26/27 margin guidance); Sept-26 Varian platform; H1 FY26/27 print Dec-26 (China + Evo orders).

sub.text: Catalyst density is good (4 events in 7 months). __Each can downgrade or upgrade case decisively.__ This is the right surface area for monitoring; wrong surface area for entry pre-resolution.

blocks.text: _P4 = case-quality / bankability assessment. 8 building blocks. ESA finds material distribution shift downward (BB#2 + BB#4 + BB#6 all D; BB#1 + BB#8 down to C; BB#5 + BB#7 hold; BB#3 deteriorating). P4 rating held at C but right at the C/D boundary._

items.text: **BB#1 verdict — DOWNGRADE B→C.** Triaging-stage simplicity ('triple-lever margin recovery') survives at headline level but ESA depth surfaces 3 hidden complexity drivers.

sub.text: Hidden complexity 1: R&D cap unwinding 60% → 40% creates 150bp persistent EBIT drag — not visible in management's 14% target headline.
Hidden complexity 2: Backlog quality is now a __binary gate__ (one more write-down → cascade) rather than a steady-state metric.
Hidden complexity 3: Varian Sept-26 timing creates a __step-change risk__ rather than smooth competitive intensity — case has discontinuous downside.

items.text: **RA1 (DOUBLE) Required simplicity guardrails.** ESA reveals case requires holding 3 guardrails simultaneously — each holds well; their conjunction is fragile.

sub.text: Guardrail 1: cost-savings flow through (high-confidence, ~85%).
Guardrail 2: order-book stabilises (medium, ~60% — needs 2 more clean Qs).
Guardrail 3: Varian Sept-26 platform doesn't crush Evo (medium-low, ~40%).
Conjunction probability: __0.85 × 0.60 × 0.40 = ~20%__. Not the simple bull case it appears.

blocks.text: _BB#2 Foundation Quality is the QUADRUPLED-floor family at ESA (per memo doctrine v3.4 §IV.F — 780w target). It examines the bedrock structural quality of the business and its ecosystem. ESA depth surfaces __structural disadvantages__ vs Varian (post-Siemens bundling) that cost-cutting cannot solve. Triaging C → ESA D._

items.text: **BB#2 verdict — DOWNGRADE C→D.** Three structural Foundation issues compound: (i) Varian-Siemens bundle moat, (ii) Philips magnet single-source dependency on Unity, (iii) US share erosion 20% → <15% structural not

sub.text: cyclical.

Foundation issue 1 (Varian bundle): Post-2021 Siemens acquisition gives Varian linac + diagnostic imaging + ARIA OIS bundle for enterprise hospital tenders. EKTA cannot match. This is __structural, not solvable by Evo or by cost cuts__.
Foundation issue 2 (Philips magnet): Unity (1.5T MR-linac) sources magnets EXCLUSIVELY from Philips. Permanent BoM cost cap. Limits Unity's GM upside even as installed base grows.
Foundation issue 3 (US share): 20% → <15% over 3Y. Bundle structurally favours Varian; Evo arrival arrests but does not reverse.

items.text: **TC1 Duopoly structure stable? C.** Duopoly intact (97% radio-surgery share between Varian + Elekta) — but Elekta's share within the duopoly is shrinking.

sub.text: Global market share: Varian/Siemens ~60%; Elekta ~25%; rest ~15% (Accuray, Mevion, Brainlab niches). Stable at the duopoly level.
__But the duopoly is asymmetric and getting more so.__ Varian's bundle pulls share within the duopoly; Elekta's response is product-by-product (Evo, Unity) not bundle-wide.

items.text: **TC2 Installed base sticky? B.** 7,500 installed devices, 14-yr asset life, MOSAIQ OIS lock-in, 43% recurring service revenue.

sub.text: Service attachment >85% on installed base — high ARPU, sticky. This is the primary defensive asset.
Replacement cycles favour incumbent: 70-80% of replacement orders go to existing vendor (workflow, training, integration costs).

items.text: **TC3 Backlog quality? D.** SEK 7.1bn write-downs across Q1+Q2 FY25/26 (≈39% of annual revenue). Q3 = first clean quarter. **Cockroach rule applies.**

sub.text: Per APM SKILL §8 ACH Threshold and §Cockroach Pattern: 2 consecutive write-down quarters = cockroach pattern. Discipline: assume more cockroaches until 3+ clean quarters.
Remaining backlog SEK 34-40bn — no independent verification of quality. Bear: further write-downs material risk (~25%). Bull: Q3 clean print is the turn.
__Backlog quality cascades into D.II.1 ACH#11 (NEW invalidation trigger)__: third write-down → automatic park or kill.

items.text: **TC4 ROIC + capital efficiency? C.** ROIC ~12% FY24/25 (improving from 9% trough); capital efficiency improving via NWC -9%, but R&D cap unwinding masks underlying ROIC trend.

sub.text: Underlying ROIC (ex-R&D-cap-effect) is rising; reported ROIC will be flat-to-down for 2-3Y as capitalised projects amortise. __Optical headwind is real.__

items.text: **TC5 Moat trajectory? D — narrowing.** Varian bundle is widening its moat; Elekta's responses (Evo, Unity, AI software) are product-level not bundle-level.

sub.text: Hospital consolidation (US health systems merging) increases scale advantage of Varian's bundle. Elekta wins specialist tenders (academic medical centres, centres of excellence) but loses system-wide bids.
__The moat-narrowing is structural and trend-rising.__ Net new orders systematically favour Varian in consolidating buyer environments.

items.text: **TC6 Net Foundation? C → D.** Sticky installed base (TC2 B) is the high-quality asset; backlog quality (TC3 D) and moat trajectory (TC5 D) are the low-quality liabilities. Net:

sub.text: __Foundation deteriorating, not stable__.
